1.1: The Proverbs of Solomon, son of David, King of Israel.
1.2: To know wisdom and instruction and to notice the words of intelligence;
1.3: To receive instruction and awe and righteousness and judgment and uprightness;
1.4: To give children cunning and to boys knowledge and intelligence.
1.5: A wise man will listen and will add to his wisdom and the intelligent will gain leadership
1.6: To perceive proverbs and parables and the words of the wise and their riddles.
1.7: The chief wisdom is the awe of LORD JEHOVAH, but the evil despise knowledge and instruction.
1.8: Hear, my son, the law of your father and do not forget the law of your mother.
1.9: For they are beauty to your head and a necklace for your neck.
1.10: My son if sinners entice you, do not consent,
1.11: And if they say to you "Come with us, let us lay wait for blood and hide for the innocent in depravity
1.12: And we will swallow him alive like Sheol, and one without blemish like those descending into the pit;
1.13: We will find all his wealth and his stuff and fill our houses with loot;
1.14: Cast your lot with us and each of us will have a money bag."
1.15: My son, do not go in the way with them, but withhold your foot from their paths,
1.16: Because their feet run to evil and hasten to shed innocent blood.
1.17: It is by deceit that nets are spread for a bird.
1.18: And they lay wait for blood and hide themselves.
1.19: So are the ways of all who do evil and take the lives of their Masters.
1.20: Wisdom is praised in the market and raises her voice in the street.
1.21: She preaches in the chief streets and she speaks her words in the entrances of the city gates.
1.22: How long, children, will you love childishness, and you mockers desire mockery, and you fools hate knowledge?
1.23: If you turn to my reproof, I shall bring forth my Spirit to you, and I shall show you my word.
1.24: For I have cried, and you have not believed, and I have lifted my voice and you have not listened.
1.25: And you despise all my counsels and you were not pleased with my reproof.
1.26: I also shall laugh over your destruction and I shall rejoice when tumult and destruction suddenly comes upon you.
1.27: And your destruction will come like a hurricane, and when trouble and adversity comes against them,
1.28: Then they will call on me and I shall not answer them, and they will come early to my presence and they will not find me
1.29: Because they hated knowledge and they did not choose the worship of LORD JEHOVAH.
1.30: And they did not choose my counsel and they have rejected all my reproof.
1.31: And they will eat of the fruit of their ways and they will be filled with their counsels.
1.32: Because of this, the turning away of the children will kill them, and the error of feeble minds will destroy them.
1.33: And he who hears me will dwell in hope and will be quiet from the multitude of evils.

2.1: My son, if you receive my words, and hide my commandments in your heart
2.2: And you will incline your ear to wisdom and turn your heart to understanding
2.3: And call to understanding and lift up your voice to intelligence
2.4: And you will seek it as silver and you will search it out as treasure
2.5: Then you will understand the awe of LORD JEHOVAH and you will find the knowledge of God.
2.6: Because LORD JEHOVAH gives wisdom, and from his mouth, knowledge and understanding.
2.7: And he keeps hope for those who are upright and helps those who walk without blemish.
2.8: And he keeps the ways of justice; the ways of his pure ones he will preserve.
2.9: Then you will understand righteousness and justice and uprightness of all excellent ways
2.10: When wisdom will enter your heart and knowledge will be sweet to your soul.
2.11: A good mind will keep you and the intelligence of the good will deliver you
2.12: That you may be delivered from evil ways and from men speaking contradictions
2.13: Who leave the upright way and they walk in the way of darkness.
2.14: And they rejoice to do evil and they rejoice in the lifestyle of the evil.
2.15: Their ways are perverted and their paths are crooked.
2.16: Wisdom will deliver you from an estranged woman who subverts by her words
2.17: Who has left the rearing of her childhood and has forgotten the covenant of her God.
2.18: She has forgotten the doorposts of her house and the steps of her paths.
2.19: None who enter unto her return, neither remember the way of life.
2.20: Walk therefore in the way of the good and keep the way of the righteous ones
2.21: Because the upright dwell in the land and those without blemish are spared in it
2.22: And dissenters are consumed from the Earth and the evil are uprooted from it.

3.1: My son, do not forget my law and let your heart keep my commandments
3.2: Because the length of days and the years of life they will add to you.
3.3: And let not peace, favor and truth forsake you; bind them on your neck and write them upon the tablet of your heart
3.4: That you may find mercies and grace and intelligence in the presence of God and in the presence of the children of men.
3.5: Hope in LORD JEHOVAH from your whole heart and do not trust upon the wisdom of your soul.
3.6: Know him in all your ways and he straightens your paths.
3.7: Do not be wise in your own eyes but be in awe of LORD JEHOVAH and depart from evil
3.8: That there may be healing to your flesh and fatness to your bones.
3.9: Honor LORD JEHOVAH from your possessions and from the best of all your increase
3.10: And your barns will be filled with fullness and your wine presses will pour forth wine.
3.11: My son, do not reject the discipline of LORD JEHOVAH and do not neglect his admonition to you
3.12: Because the one whom LORD JEHOVAH loves, he disciplines, as a father disciplines his son.
3.13: Blessed is the man who finds wisdom and the son of man who finds understanding
3.14: Because its merchandise is better than the merchandise of silver, and the excellencies of its increase than refined gold,
3.15: For it is more valuable than precious stones and nothing is comparable to it.
3.16: The length of days is in her right hand; wealth and honor in her left.
3.17: Her ways are the ways of sweetness and all her paths are peace.
3.18: She is a tree of life to those who hold her fast and blessed are those who look for her.
3.19: LORD JEHOVAH in his wisdom laid the foundations of the Earth and the Heavens were established by his understanding;
3.20: And in his knowledge the depths were broken up and the clouds sprinkle dew.
3.21: My son, let not your eyes depart from keeping my teaching and my counsels.
3.22: And it shall be Life to your soul and Grace to your neck.
3.23: And then you will walk your ways in hope and you will not stumble by your foot.
3.24: And you will sleep and be unafraid, and you will lie down, and sleep will be sweet to you.
3.25: And you will not be afraid of trouble that comes suddenly, or of the rush of sinners when it comes.
3.26: Because LORD JEHOVAH shall be with you and will keep your foot from being caught.
3.27: Do not refrain from doing what is beautiful as long as you are able.
3.28: And when you have possessions, do not say to your neighbor, "Go, and come again tomorrow, and I shall give to you."
3.29: Do not devise evil against your neighbor who sits with you in the quiet.
3.30: Do not judge with a man by deceit,
3.31: And do not envy an evil man, neither desire any of his ways;
3.32: Because an evil one is defiled before LORD JEHOVAH and the discourses of LORD JEHOVAH are with the upright.
3.33: The curse of LORD JEHOVAH is in the houses of the evil and he will bless the dwellings of the righteous.
3.34: And he will overthrow mockers, and he will hover over the wise.
3.35: The wise will inherit honor and fools will receive disgrace.

4.1: Hear, children, the instruction of a father, and listen to knowledge and understanding.
4.2: Because I have given you good teaching, forsake not my law.
4.3: For I have been a delightful son to my father and an only child in the presence of my mother.
4.4: And he taught me and said to me, "Confirm my word in your heart; keep my commandments and live, and my law like the pupils of the eyes.”
4.5: Get wisdom and get understanding, and do not turn aside from the sayings of my mouth.
4.6: Do not forsake her that she may keep you; love her that she may deliver you.
4.7: Wisdom is the summit; get wisdom, and with all your possessions purchase understanding.
4.8: Love her that she may exalt you and embrace her that she may honor you.
4.9: And she will put the beauty of grace on your head and she will satisfy you with a crown of glory.
4.10: Hear, my son, and receive my words, and the years of your life will be many.
4.11: I have taught you the ways of wisdom and I have led you in the straight paths.
4.12: And when you walk, your walking will not quake, and if you run, you will not stumble.
4.13: Hold my instruction fast and do not forsake her; keep her because she is your life.
4.14: Do not go in the way of sinners and do not envy the way of the evil.
4.15: Do not pass through the place where they dwell, but turn aside and pass from it.
4.16: They do not sleep until they do evil and their sleep flees away until they do their desires.
4.17: Because their food is the food of evil and they drink the wine of depravity.
4.18: But the way of the righteous ones is like the shining light, and the light progresses until the day is established.
4.19: But the way of the wicked is darkness and they do not know on what they stumble.
4.20: My son, give ear to my words and incline your ear to my speech.
4.21: Do not let them depart before your eyes but keep them within your heart.
4.22: Because he who finds them has life, and all healing in his flesh.
4.23: Keep your heart with all caution because from it is the outgoing of life.
4.24: Cause the perverse mouth to pass from you, and put far from your lips the thought of depravity.
4.25: Let your eyes see in uprightness and let your eyelashes be straight before you.
4.26: Let your foot pass from evil streets and all your ways will be established.
4.27: Do not turn aside to the right or to the left, but remove your foot from evil.

5.1: My son, give ear to my wisdom and incline your ear to my sayings
5.2: So that you heed counsel and your lips will keep knowledge.
5.3: Because the lips of an estranged woman are dripping honey and her words are softer than oil.
5.4: But their end is of bitter wormwood, for they are sharper than a doubleedged sword
5.5: And her legs marry to death; her walk causes men to recline in Sheol.
5.6: And she treads not in the way of life, for her paths lead them astray and they are unknown.
5.7: Therefore children, hear me, and do not turn away from the words of my mouth.
5.8: Remove your way far from her and do not go near the door of her house
5.9: Lest you give others your power and your years to those who are merciless
5.10: And foreigners will be filled with your power and your labor enter into the house of strangers
5.11: And your soul moves you to regret in your old age when the flesh of your body is wasted
5.12: And you will say, "Why have I hated instruction and my heart has despised reproof"
5.13: And I have not listened to the voice of my teachers, and to my instructors have not turned my ear?
5.14: I have been in almost every evil in the assembly and in the congregation.
5.15: Drink waters from your well and running waters from your spring,
5.16: And let your waters overflow in your streets and let them travel in your streets.
5.17: And let them be to you alone, and let not estranged males be sexual partners with you.
5.18: And your fountain shall be blessed and rejoice with the wife of your youth
5.19: An affectionate hind and an affectionate mountain goat; learn her ways always and think on her love.
5.20: Do not go astray, my son, with an estranged woman, neither embrace the bosom of an estranged woman.
5.21: Because the ways of a man are in front of LORD JEHOVAH and all his paths are open before him.
5.22: The evil man will be held captive in his debts and he will be bound in the cords of his sins.
5.23: He will die without instruction and in the abundance of his error he will be led astray.

6.1: My son, if you are security for your friend, you have yielded your hand to a stranger.
6.2: You are trapped by the saying of your mouth and you are taken by the speech of your lips.
6.3: Do thus my son and be delivered because you are security for the person of your friend; you have fallen into the hands of your enemy. Coax therefore your friend, for whom you are security
6.4: And you shall not give sleep to your eyes neither slumber to your eyelids
6.5: That you may be delivered like a deer from a net and like a bird from a snare.
6.6: Be like the ant and see her ways and learn, for there is no harvest for her,
6.7: And there is no ruler over her, neither anyone to drive her.
6.8: And she prepares for herself her bread from the summer and she stores her food at harvest.
6.9: How long will you sleep, sluggard? When will you arise from your sleep?
6.10: You will slumber a little, and a little you will sleep, and for a little you will put your hand upon your chest.
6.11: Poverty will come upon you and want will overtake you like an athletic man.
6.12: A foolish evil man walks in rebellion.
6.13: He winks with his eyes and strikes with his feet and he signals with his fingers,
6.14: And he is turned backward in his heart and devises evil always, and he stirs up strife between two.
6.15: Because of this, his ruin will come suddenly and suddenly he will be broken, and there will be no remedy for him.
6.16: There are six things that LORD JEHOVAH hates, and his soul has despised a seventh:
6.17: Lofty eyes and the false tongue, hands that shed innocent blood,
6.18: The heart that makes plans of depravity and feet that hasten to run to evil,
6.19: A false witness who speaks lies and deception among brothers.
6.20: Keep, my son, the commandment of your father and do not forget the law of your mother.
6.21: Fasten them in your heart permanently and coil them around your neck.
6.22: And whenever you walk, make them cleave to you and they will be with you; keep them that they may keep you, and when you are awakened, they will be your meditation.
6.23: For the commandment is a lamp and the law is a light, and reproof is the way of life and instruction,
6.24: To keep you from the evil woman and from the slander of the tongue of the estranged woman.
6.25: Do not desire her beauty in your heart and do not be ensnared by her eyes, neither let her capture you with her eyelids.
6.26: For the comparison of a woman who is a harlot is like a loaf of bread, and a man's wife hunts the precious soul.
6.27: Does a man put fire in his bosom and do his garments not burn?
6.28: Or does a man walk upon coals of fire and his feet are unharmed?
6.29: So he who goes unto the wife of his neighbor and touches her will not be innocent.
6.30: And not to wonder at one who is caught stealing, because he steals to satisfy himself when he is hungry.
6.31: And if he is caught stealing, he pays each sevenfold, and he gives all the possessions of his house.
6.32: But he who commits adultery with a woman lacks intelligence and is destroying his soul.
6.33: And he is working to bring disgrace to himself and it apprehends him, and his shame will not be forgotten.
6.34: For because the anger of a husband is full of jealousy, he has no pity in the day of vengeance.
6.35: He does not accept the appearance of a gift, neither does he consent when you increase bribes to him.

7.1: My son, keep my words and hide my commandments in your heart.
7.2: Keep my commandments and live, and my law like the pupil of the eye.
7.3: Tie them on your neck and write them upon the tablets of your heart.
7.4: Say to wisdom, " You are my sister”, and to understanding, "My expounder of knowledge.”
7.5: For it will keep you from the estranged woman whose words are seductive.
7.6: Because she looked out from a window of her house and from the balcony
7.7: And she saw children and gazed on the boys and on the feebleminded
7.8: Who pass by in the street, beside the corner of the road of her house.
7.9: At evening, at setting of the sun, in the darkness of the night and in thick darkness
7.10: A woman went forth to meet him in the attire of a harlot to make the young men's hearts flutter.
7.11: She is rebellious and a prodigal, and her feet do not rest in her house,
7.12: But she wanders outside from time to time in the streets, and she lies in wait on the corners.
7.13: And she caught him and kissed him, and her face was bold, and she said to him:
7.14: There are peace sacrifices today; I have paid my vows,
7.15: Therefore I went out to meet you, for I've been waiting to see you and I found you.
7.16: I have spread my bed on a carpet and with Egyptian bedspreads I have spread it;
7.17: I have sprinkled myrrh on my bed and saffron crocus and cinnamon.
7.18: Come let us delight in love until the morning and we shall embrace each other in desire,
7.19: For my husband has not been in the house; he is gone on the road far away.
7.20: He has taken the money bag of money in his hand and it will be for many days before he comes to his house.
7.21: And she deceived him with the multitude of her words; in the flattery of her lips unto him, she enticed him.
7.22: And he went after her like a child, and like a bull that goes with a butcher, and like a dog to captivity.
7.23: And like a stag when an arrow flies into its liver, he hastens like a living bird to a snare, and does not know that it is to the death of his soul that he goes.
7.24: Therefore, children, hear me and obey the words of my mouth.
7.25: Do not incline your heart to her ways and do not go astray in her paths
7.26: Because she has cast down a multitude of the slain and mighty ones; they all have been killed by her.
7.27: The paths of her house are the paths of Sheol, of those descending to the chambers of death.

8.1: Because of this, wisdom preaches, and understanding will answer you.
8.2: For on top of the high places is wisdom; between the roads and upon the side of the paths she stands.
8.3: Over the gates with her mouth she calls, and in the entrances of the gates of The Glorious City.
8.4: And she says, "To you, men, I call, and my voice to the children of men"
8.5: That children may understand subtlety and the feeble minds may know in their heart.
8.6: Hear me, because it is truth that I speak, and my mouth opens with uprightness.
8.7: For my mouth ruminates truth and lying lips are unclean before me.
8.8: And all these words of my mouth are in righteousness and there is no perversion or crookedness in them.
8.9: All these are plain to him who perceives them and they are right to him who chooses to know them.
8.10: Receive discipline and not silver, and choose for yourselves knowledge rather than refined gold.
8.11: "Because wisdom is better than refined gold and better than precious stones, and nothing compares to it.”
8.12: I, Wisdom, have created cunning and I own knowledge and reason.
8.13: The worship of LORD JEHOVAH is hating evil; pomp, pride, the evil way, and the perverse mouth, I hate.
8.14: Reason is mine and instruction; mine is understanding and mine is manly strength.
8.15: Because of me, Kings rule, and Rulers search out righteousness.
8.16: Because of me Rulers are authorized and Nobles and all Judges of righteousness.
8.17: I love my friends, and those who seek me will find me.
8.18: Riches and honor are mine, and possessions, old age and righteousness.
8.19: My fruits are better than refined gold and my produce than choice silver.
8.20: I walk in the way of righteousness and within the streets of judgment
8.21: That I might leave hope as an inheritance to my friends, and I shall fill their treasures.
8.22: LORD JEHOVAH created me at the beginning of his creation and from before all his works.
8.23: And before the world he was possessed by me, and from the beginning, before he would establish the Earth.
8.24: Before the depths would be, I was born, and before there would be waters in the springs.
8.25: And before the mountains would be established, and before the hills, I was formed in the womb.
8.26: Before he would make the Earth and the floods and the beginning of the dust of the world
8.27: When the Heavens were established, I was with him, and when he made a circle over the face of the depths.
8.28: And when he empowered the clouds from above and when he strengthened the springs of the depths,
8.29: When he set the law of the Sea and the waters would not disobey his mouth, when he made the foundations of the Earth,
8.30: I was fashioning with him; he was rejoicing in me every day, and I have been rejoicing before him always.
8.31: I have been rejoicing in the world of his Earth and I have been glorying in the sons of man.
8.32: Therefore, children, hear me: Blessed is he who keeps my ways.
8.33: Hear instruction and receive wisdom and do not go astray.
8.34: Blessed is the man who will listen to me and will keep watch upon my gates all day and guards the posts of my gates.
8.35: Because my goings forth are the goings forth of Life, and so the will of LORD JEHOVAH goes forth.
8.36: And those who sin against me harm their soul, and all who hate me are the friends of death.

9.1: Wisdom has built a house and she has set seven pillars in it.
9.2: And she has killed her sacrificial victim and she has mingled her wine and has prepared her table.
9.3: And she has sent her Servants to call upon the hills and they will say:
9.4: Who is simpleminded? Let him come to me, and whoever lacks his mind; And he said to him:
9.5: Come, eat of my bread and drink of the wine which I have mingled
9.6: Put off from you the deficiency of your mind, and live, and agree with the way of uprightness.
9.7: To an evil person, discipline gives disgrace, and it rebukes the vice of the evil one.
9.8: Do not rebuke an evil one, lest he hate you, but rebuke a wise one, for he will love you.
9.9: Give opportunity to a wise man that again he may learn wisdom, and teach the righteous that he may progress beyond his teacher.
9.10: The beginning of wisdom is the awe of LORD JEHOVAH and the knowledge of the Righteous Ones is understanding;
9.11: Because by it, your days will be multiplied, and years of life will be added to you.
9.12: My son, if you are wise, you are wise for yourself and for your friends, and if you will be evil, you alone will draw out your evils. The one who denies falsely, feeds on the winds and pursues a bird of the sky, for he has forsaken his service to travel in the desert without water, and from beside the path of his vineyard, and he forgets the trodden paths; he travels in thirst, and he will gather nothing!
9.13: A woman deficient in mind is alluring and she knows no shame.
9.14: She sits at the door of her house upon a high seat.
9.15: She calls passersby in the way who go straight on their ways and she says:
9.16: Who is it that is simple? Let him come to me, and he that is deficient in his mind. And one says to her:
9.17: Stolen waters are sweet, and secret bread is pleasant,
9.18: And he does not know that mighty men perish in her presence, and all that are summoned to her are in the depths of Sheol; but do not tarry in that place, and do not let your eye be caught by her, for so you should pass by the waters of strangers and the river of foreigners, and from the waters of foreigners you should not drink, so that many days and years of life will be added to you.

10.1: A wise son gladdens his father, and a son deficient in mind shames his mother.
10.2: There is no profit in the treasures of evil, but righteousness saves from death.
10.3: For LORD JEHOVAH will not famish the soul of the righteous, but the property of the evil he demolishes.
10.4: Poverty humbles a man but the hands of the diligent make rich.
10.5: He that works in summer is an intelligent son, and he who sleeps in harvest is a son that brings shame.
10.6: Blessing will be upon the head of the righteous and the mouth of the evil will conceal evil.
10.7: The memory of the righteous is for a blessing and the name of the evil will be extinguished.
10.8: For the wise will receive the commandment to his heart and he who is insane is caught by his lips.
10.9: He that walks in perfection goes in hope, and he who perverts his ways will be known.
10.10: He that winks with his eyes in deceit gives sorrow, and he who reproves openly makes peace.
10.11: The mouth of the righteous is a fountain of life, and the mouths of the evil will hide evil.
10.12: Hatred stirs up judgment, and shame will cover all the evil ones.
10.13: Whoever brings forth wisdom from his lips beats the man lacking a heart with a staff.
10.14: The wise will conceal knowledge and the mouth of the hasty is near ruin.
10.15: The property of the rich is mighty fortress cities, and the ruin of the poor is their poverty.
10.16: The work of the Righteous One is for life and the fruits of the Evil One are for sin.
10.17: The way of life preserves instruction and he that hates rebuke is a moron.
10.18: The lips of the evil lie in wait in hostility and he that brings forth a curse is lacking a mind.
10.19: In the multitude of words the evil one is not saved and he who restrains his lips is intelligent.
10.20: The tongue of the righteous is choice silver and the heart of the evil is bitterness.
10.21: The lips of the righteous multiply compassion and the insane will die in the deficiency of their mind.
10.22: The blessing of LORD JEHOVAH makes rich and there shall be no sorrow with it.
10.23: A moron, in making merry, does evil, and a man has wisdom by his reasoning.
10.24: The evil one is dragged to destruction and hope is given to the righteous.
10.25: As the tornado passes suddenly so the evil one will be destroyed and not be found, but the foundation of the righteous one will be laid for eternity.
10.26: As unripe grapes hurt the teeth, and smoke the eyes, so evil hurts those practicing it.
10.27: The worship of LORD JEHOVAH will add days, and the years of the evil ones will be subtracted.
10.28: The hope of the righteous is with joy and the hope of the evil will perish.
10.29: The way of LORD JEHOVAH is power to the perfect, and ruin to evildoers.
10.30: The righteous will not be moved for eternity and the evil will not dwell in the Earth.
10.31: The mouth of the righteous speaks wisdom and a perverted tongue will be cut out.
10.32: The lips of the righteous know goodness, and the mouths of the evil are perverted.

11.1: LORD JEHOVAH despises the scales of deceit and he is pleased with correct scales.
11.2: Where corruption enters, disgrace enters, and wisdom is for the humble.
11.3: The hope of the upright will be built up, and the pride of the evil will be overthrown.
11.4: Wealth is not useful in the day of wrath but righteousness saves from death.
11.5: The righteousness of the perfect straightens his way and the evil one has fallen in his evil.
11.6: The righteousness of the upright will save them, and the evil will be captured in their evil.
11.7: Whenever an evil man has died, his hope has perished, and the hope of the evil perishes.
11.8: The righteous is delivered from trouble and the evil one will enter in his place.
11.9: With his mouth an evil one destroys his neighbor, and by knowledge the righteous shall be strengthened.
11.10: By the blessing of the righteous the city will be strong, and in the destruction of the evil it will rejoice.
11.11: The city will be exalted by the blessings of the upright and by the mouth of the evil it will be overthrown.
11.12: He that despises his neighbor is deficient in mind and a man of understanding dwells in quietness.
11.13: An adversary reveals a secret, and he who is faithful in his spirit will hide the matter.
11.14: A people that has no leadership will fall and its salvation is in much counsel.
11.15: The evil one does evil when he meets the righteous, because he hates those who await good news.
11.16: A loving woman upholds honor for the husband, but the woman who hates the truth is a throne of disgrace. Lazy men, even in their wealth, are needy, and strong men uphold knowledge.
11.17: A pure man lays down a good reward for his soul and an unmerciful man destroys his body.
11.18: The wicked does works of depravity, and truth is the reward of him who sows righteousness.
11.19: A son of righteousness has life and he who does evil has death.
11.20: LORD JEHOVAH despises the perverse heart and he delights in those who are without blemish in the way.
11.21: He that stretches his hand against his neighbor will not be innocent of evil, and the seed of the righteous will be delivered.
11.22: Like a nose ring of gold in the snout of the pig, so is a beautiful woman who has turned from discretion.
11.23: The desire of the righteous is in excellent things, and the hope of the evil, in sensual passion.
11.24: There is one that distributes seed and brings in much, and there is one who does not gather his own and has little.
11.25: The blessed soul will grow fat and an accursed one will again be cursed.
11.26: He that withholds produce in affliction they will send away to his enemies, and blessing will be upon the head of him that sells.
11.27: He that seeks good seeks delight, and he that seeks evil, it will come upon him.
11.28: He that trusts upon his wealth will fall, and the righteous ones will bud like leaves.
11.29: He that builds his house with depravity will leave groanings to his children. He that does not remain quietly in his house will distribute the winds to his children and the fool will be Servant to the wise.
11.30: The fruit of the righteous is a tree of life and the souls of the evil will be removed.
11.31: If the righteous lives with hardship, where will the wicked and the sinner be found?

12.1: He that loves discipline loves knowledge, and he that hates reproof is a fool.
12.2: It will be well for the man who keeps the will of LORD JEHOVAH and an evil man will be condemned.
12.3: A son of man will not be established in evil, and the root of the righteous will not move.
12.4: A valiant wife is the crown of her husband, and as a boring worm, and a worm in wood, the woman that does evil things destroys a husband.
12.5: The purposes of the righteous are justice, and the way of life of the evil is deceit.
12.6: The words of the evil lie in ambush for blood, and the mouth of the upright delivers them.
12.7: The evil will be overthrown and they will not be found, and the house of the righteous will be established.
12.8: The children of men praise the mouth of understanding and he who lacks his reasoning will be despised.
12.9: Better is a poor man who waits on himself than one who boasts and lacks bread.
12.10: The righteous one is aware of the soul of his animal, and the evil withhold their compassions.
12.11: He that cultivates the soil will be filled with bread, and he that runs after futility is devoid of reason.
12.12: The evil one lusts to do evil, and the root of the righteous ones will sprout.
12.13: The evil one is caught in the evil of his lips and the righteous escapes from trouble.
12.14: A good man will be satisfied from the fruit of his mouth and every son of man is rewarded according to the work of his hands.
12.15: The way of the fool is beautiful in his eyes, and he that listens to counsel is wise.
12.16: A fool immediately makes his wrath known and a cunning one hides his insult.
12.17: The righteous speaks obvious trustworthiness and the testimony of the evil is deceit.
12.18: There are those that speak a stabbing sword, but the tongue of the wise heals.
12.19: The lips of truth are right, and the hasty witness does evil with his tongue.
12.20: Deceit in the heart of the evil devises evil, and those that counsel peace will have joy.
12.21: Nothing depraved is good to the righteous man, and the evil are filled with evil.
12.22: LORD JEHOVAH despises false lips and he delights in those who practice faith.
12.23: A cunning man is a seat of knowledge, and the heart of fools proclaims evil.
12.24: The hand of the mighty will rule and the deceitful will be under tribute.
12.25: A fearful word troubles the heart of a man and a good word gladdens it.
12.26: The righteous gives good counsel to his friend and the way of the evil will mislead them.
12.27: Prey will not be taken for the deceitful man, but the precious possession of a man is choice.
12.28: In the way of righteousness is life, and the way of the angry is unto death.

13.1: A wise son hears his father and an evil son who does not receive reproof will perish.
13.2: A good man will be satisfied from the fruit of his mouth and the souls of the evil will be destroyed.
13.3: He that keeps his mouth takes care of his soul, and he that opens his lips works shipwreck to his soul.
13.4: A man that does not work always falls into lusts and the soul of the strong will grow fat.
13.5: The righteous hates a lying word, and the evil will be disgraced and put to shame.
13.6: Righteousness will keep him who is perfect in his way, and the sinner's sin will destroy him.
13.7: There are those who enrich themselves and have nothing, and there is one who makes himself poor and possesses an abundance.
13.8: The salvation of a man's soul is his wealth and the poor does not accept condemnation.
13.9: The light of the righteous will exult and the lamp of the evil will be put out.
13.10: An evil man in disgrace does evil, and those who are counseled are wise.
13.11: Property that comes by evil will decrease and he that gathers by righteousness will increase.
13.12: A man who begins to help is better than he that props up with hope, and the tree of life brings hope.
13.13: He that has contempt against the word will be destroyed by it, and he that is in awe of the commandment will be saved; there will be no favor to the deceitful man, and a wise man whose works are upright will lift up the head by his way.
13.14: The law of wisdom is a fountain of life to those who turn from the snares of death.
13.15: Good understanding gives compassion, and the way of the evil is unto destruction.
13.16: Everything that a wise one does is in knowledge, and a fool speaks emptiness.
13.17: An evil messenger falls into affliction and a trustworthy messenger is a healer.
13.18: Poverty and shame nullify instruction and he that keeps reproof will be praised.
13.19: An honorable desire will be sweet to the soul and the defilement of the evil is removed from knowledge.
13.20: He that walks with the wise will be wise, and he that walks with a fool will harm himself.
13.21: Sinners will pursue affliction, and the righteous ones will be given precious things.
13.22: A good man gives an inheritance to his grandchildren and the wealth of sinners is kept for the righteous.
13.23: Those who do not see the way of life for themselves destroy many years of wealth, and some are destroyed utterly.
13.24: Everyone that spares concerning his rod, hates his son, and he that loves his son disciplines him earnestly.
13.25: The righteous eats and satisfies his soul, and the belly of the evil will want.

14.1: A wise woman builds a house and the foolish woman destroys it with her hands.
14.2: He that walks in uprightness is in awe of LORD JEHOVAH and he that is shifty in his way despises him.
14.3: Provocation and disgrace are in the mouth of the fool, and the lips of the wise will preserve them.
14.4: Where there are no oxen, the stalls are clean, and the abundance of crops is by the power of the ox.
14.5: A trustworthy witness is not false and he that speaks falsehood is a false witness.
14.6: The evil one seeks wisdom and will not find it, and knowledge is found by the intelligent.
14.7: To the foolish man, everything is against him, and the lips of the wise are the weapons of knowledge.
14.8: A cunning one in his wisdom understands his way, and the way of fools is in error.
14.9: The households of the evil are rightly begging for cleansing, and the households of the righteous are acceptable.
14.10: Fools commit sins and upright children are willing.
14.11: The understanding heart always has sorrow to its soul and a stranger will not share in its joy. The household of the evil will be destroyed and the tent of the upright will exult.
14.12: There is a way that men think is right, and its ways are the ways of death.
14.13: Even in laughter the heart will sorrow, and the end of its joy is grief.
14.14: He who is rash in his heart will be filled with his ways, and a good man will be filled from the awe of his soul.
14.15: A child believes every word, and a cunning one distinguishes good from evil.
14.16: A wise one is afraid and departs from evil, and the fool partakes in it confidently.
14.17: Nothing a hasty man does is advised and a wise man is temperate.
14.18: Fools inherit madness and the intelligent distribute knowledge.
14.19: The evil will fall in front of the good and the evil will come to the door of the righteous.
14.20: The poor is hated by his neighbors and the friends of the rich are many.
14.21: He that despises his neighbor sins, and he that shows mercy upon the poor is blessed.
14.22: The evil go astray doing evil, and the compassionate and righteous ones do all good things; evildoers do not understand mercy and faith, but compassion and faith are with doers of good.
14.23: In all that concerns you, one thing is profitable, and he that is diminished in his way of life is quiet and will be pleasant. LORD JEHOVAH heals every sickness, and the speech of the lips of the evil damages them.
14.24: A crown of the wise is their wealth, and the way of life of fools is their insanity.
14.25: A true witness delivers souls, and a false witness speaks deceit.
14.26: The awe of LORD JEHOVAH is strong in hope and he will be a protector to his children.
14.27: The awe of LORD JEHOVAH is a fountain of life to those that turn from the snares of death.
14.28: By many people the king is honored, and by the end of the people the king is shattered.
14.29: He that is patient is very wise and he that is impatient, very foolish.
14.30: He that cools his anger is a healer of his heart, and envy is the decay of the bones.
14.31: He that oppresses the poor is very evil; he angers him who made him; he that honors LORD JEHOVAH shows mercy upon the afflicted.
14.32: The evil one is overthrown by his evil, and he that trusts that he has no sin is righteous.
14.33: In the heart of the righteous dwells wisdom, and in the heart of fools it will not be known.
14.34: Righteousness will exalt the people and sin diminishes a people.
14.35: The pleasure of the King is with an understanding Servant, and by the wisdom of his soul evil is overthrown. He that trusts that he has no sins is righteous, but passion will destroy even wise men.

15.1: A soft word turns away anger and a hard word arouses wrath.
15.2: The tongue of wisdom adorns knowledge, and the mouth of fools vomits a curse.
15.3: The eyes of LORD JEHOVAH are in every place, gazing on the good and on the evil.
15.4: The tree of life is the healing of the tongue, and he that eats its fruit will be filled by it.
15.5: A fool mocks at the instruction of his father and he that is smart keeps reproof.
15.6: In the house of the righteous is much power, and the produce of the evil will be destroyed.
15.7: The lips of the wise speak knowledge and the hearts of fools are unrighteous.
15.8: LORD JEHOVAH despises the sacrifices of the evil and his pleasure is in the prayer of the upright.
15.9: LORD JEHOVAH despises the way of the evil and he shows mercy to him who practices righteousness.
15.10: The instruction of those who do not know evil is revealed, and those who hate reproof will die.
15.11: Sheol and destruction are in front of LORD JEHOVAH, and also the hearts of the children of men.
15.12: The evil one does not love him that rebukes him, and he does not walk with the wise.
15.13: A glad heart improves the body and the depressed heart saddens the spirit.
15.14: The heart of the righteous seeks knowledge, and the mouth of the evil speaks evil things.
15.15: All the days of the poor are poor, and the sweet are always calm.
15.16: To worship LORD JEHOVAH a little is better than the great treasures of the evil.
15.17: Better is a meal of vegetables and the love of The Name, than Fattened oxen with hatred.
15.18: An angry man provokes a brawl, and the patient man reproves a dispute, extinguishing it.
15.19: The ways of the lazy are filled with sorrows and the ways of the upright are clear.
15.20: A wise son gladdens his father and a foolish son is the shame of his mother.
15.21: A foolish man is lacking heart and an understanding man walks justly.
15.22: Those who honor the assembly bypass reasoning, and by the multitude of advisers advice is established.
15.23: The word of his mouth is joy to a man, and it is good for him that speaks in his time.
15.24: The Way of Life is an ascent to the understanding one, to turn away from Sheol beneath.
15.25: LORD JEHOVAH breaks down the house of the proud and he strengthens the border of the widows.
15.26: Evil ideas are detestable before LORD JEHOVAH, and pure words are sweet.
15.27: He that accepts a bribe destroys his soul and he that hates taking a bribe has Life.
15.28: The heart of the righteous meditates upon the faith, and the mouth of the evils speaks evil things.
15.29: LORD JEHOVAH is far from the evil ones and he hears the prayer of the righteous.
15.30: The light of the eyes gladdens the heart, and the good heart fattens the bones.
15.31: The ear that hears the reproof of life will lodge among the wise.
15.32: The one that detests discipline hates his soul and the one that hears correction possesses wisdom.
15.33: The awe of LORD JEHOVAH is the instruction of life and the glory of the meek one goes before him.

16.1: From a son of man is the thought of the heart and the speech of the tongue is from LORD JEHOVAH.
16.2: All the ways of a man are pure in the eyes of his soul and LORD JEHOVAH orders his path.
16.3: Show your works to LORD JEHOVAH and he will establish your thoughts.
16.4: All the works of LORD JEHOVAH are for those that submit to him, and the evil are kept for the day of evil.
16.5: Everyone that is exalted in his heart is defiled before LORD JEHOVAH, and he that stretches his hand against his neighbor will not be spared from evil.
16.6: The evil one is forgiven by grace and by truth and the awe of LORD JEHOVAH turns aside from evil.
16.7: The ways of a man are by the pleasure of LORD JEHOVAH; he also will repay his enemies.
16.8: Better is a little in righteousness than a multitude of gain which is not by justice.
16.9: The heart of a son of man plans his ways and LORD JEHOVAH orders his steps.
16.10: An oracle is upon the lips of the King, and his mouth will not err in judgment.
16.11: The weight of a balance is the judgment of LORD JEHOVAH and all his works are weights of truth.
16.12: Kings that do evil are defiled because the throne is established in righteousness.
16.13: The lips of the righteous are the pleasure of the King and he loves the word of the upright.
16.14: The anger of the King becomes messengers of death and the wise man will assuage it.
16.15: In the light of the King's face is life and his pleasure is like an early cloud.
16.16: Possessing wisdom is better than gold, and possessing understanding, better than silver.
16.17: The path of the upright turns from evil and he that pays attention to his soul keeps his way.
16.18: Disgrace is before ruin and pride of spirit before misfortune.
16.19: The humble spirit and humble eyes are better than he that divides loot with the mighty.
16.20: He that understands the word finds good, and he that trusts upon LORD JEHOVAH is blessed.
16.21: The wise of heart knows understanding, and he whose lips are sweet will increase learning.
16.22: Understanding is a fountain of life to those that know it and the instruction of fools is madness.
16.23: A wise heart knows the speech of its mouth and adds learning to its lips.
16.24: The speech of the wise is a honeycomb of honey, and it is sweet to his soul and healing to his bones.
16.25: There is a way that seems right in the eyes of the children of men, and its paths are the paths of death.
16.26: The soul of a sufferer makes him sick and from the mouth of his soul destruction comes to him.
16.27: An evil man forges evil and fire burns from his mouth.
16.28: An evil man provokes judgment and persecutes his friends without cause.
16.29: An evil man entices his neighbor and leads him in a way that is not good.
16.30: He winks with his eyes and devises perversions; he makes plans with his lips and perfects evil.
16.31: Old age is a crown of glory and the way of righteousness is praised.
16.32: He that is patient is better than a mighty man, and he that subdues himself, than he that seizes a city.
16.33: Into the breast of the vicious his portion falls; from before LORD JEHOVAH goes forth his judgment.

17.1: Dry bread in quietness is better than a house full of the sacrifices of judgment.
17.2: An intelligent Servant will be authorized over a disgraceful son, and he will divide property among brothers.
17.3: A refining pot proves silver, and the furnace gold, and LORD JEHOVAH tests the heart.
17.4: An evil man listens to the lips of the wicked, and the righteous does not hear the tongue of the wicked.
17.5: He that laughs against the poor angers his Creator, and he that rejoices at ruin will not be spared.
17.6: Grandchildren are the crown of old men and the glory of children is their fathers.
17.7: A trustworthy utterance is unbecoming to a fool; so is a false utterance to a righteous one.
17.8: A precious stone is beautiful in the eyes of him who owns it, and wherever it turns it is notable.
17.9: He that conceals an evil seeks friendship and he that hates to conceal separates from a close friend.
17.10: A rebuke breaks the heart of the wise, and instead of reproof, a fool is whipped and is unaware of it.
17.11: A contentious man seeks evil and a merciless messenger is sent upon him.
17.12: Anxiety and fear fall to a wise man, and to a fool in his insanity.
17.13: Evil will not depart from the house of him that pays evil for good.
17.14: He that sheds blood provokes judgment before a Ruler.
17.15: He that justifies the evil and condemns the righteous is defiled in front of LORD JEHOVAH.
17.16: Why does property go to a fool when he has no heart to possess wisdom?
17.17: A friend is loved at all times and a brother is born for trouble.
17.18: A foolish man hands over his bounty which he pledges for his neighbor as security.
17.19: He that loves evil loves fraud and brawling, and he that exalts his gate seeks ruin for his soul.
17.20: He that is perverse in his heart will not find good and he that perverts his tongue falls into evil.
17.21: He that begets a fool, it is to his own shame, neither will his father rejoice in him.
17.22: A merry heart improves the body and a depressed spirit dries the bones.
17.23: He that takes a bribe is evil, for he turns the way of justice aside.
17.24: The face of an intelligent one observes wisdom, and the eyes of a fool are in the depths of the Earth.
17.25: A foolish son angers his father and embitters her that bore him.
17.26: To harm the righteous is not good, neither to beat the righteous who speak correct things.
17.27: He that spares his words knows knowledge, and a patient man is wise.
17.28: A madman who is silent is considered a wise person, and he that keeps his lips silent is considered intelligent.

18.1: In his idleness one meditates on lust and mocks at good instruction.
18.2: A fool does not delight in wisdom because his heart meditates upon madness.
18.3: And when an evil one comes to the depths of evils, madness, dishonor and reproach come upon him.
18.4: The words of a man's mouth are deep waters and the fount of wisdom is a rushing torrent.
18.5: To accept the persons of the evil is not good, neither to pray for judgment upon a righteous one.
18.6: The lips of a fool bring into judgment and his mouth brings him to death.
18.7: The mouth of the fool is ruin to his soul, and by his lips his soul is caught.
18.8: The words of a lazy man cast him into evil, and they lower him to the inner chamber of Sheol.
18.9: And he whose hands are slack in his work is brother of the man who is a destroyer.
18.10: The name of LORD JEHOVAH is a strong tower; the righteous will run to it and be strengthened.
18.11: Honor and wealth are the city of his strength and his dwelling is strengthened by a city wall.
18.12: Before ruin, the heart of a man will be lifted up, and before honor is humility.
18.13: He that gives an answer before he hears is a fool, and is brought to shame.
18.14: The spirit of a man endures his suffering, but who will endure a suffering spirit?
18.15: An ordered heart possesses wisdom, and the ear of the wise listens to knowledge.
18.16: A man's gifts give him room and stand him before the great.
18.17: A man is innocent in his own judgment, and when his neighbor comes, he examines him.
18.18: Lots resolve a dispute and divide between the strong.
18.19: A brother is helped by his brother, like a city by its fortress, and they hold it like the bars of a fortress.
18.20: A man's belly will be filled with the fruit of his mouth, and by the produce of his lips he will be satisfied.
18.21: Death and life are in the power of the tongue, and he that loves it will eat from its fruit.
18.22: He that finds a good wife finds a good thing, and receives the will of LORD JEHOVAH, and he that puts away a good wife puts away good from his house.
18.23: The poor man speaks humbly and the rich man speaks hard things.
18.24: There are friends who are friends, and there is a friend that is closer than a brother.

19.1: Better is the poor that walks in integrity than the rich that perverts his ways.
19.2: It is not good for him that does not know his soul, and he that is hasty with his feet sins.
19.3: The madness of a man perverts his ways and he is enraged in his heart against LORD JEHOVAH.
19.4: Possessions add many friends and the poor is separated from his friends.
19.5: A false witness will not be spared and he that speaks falsehood will not be saved.
19.6: There are many that serve before the Prince, and he gives gifts to the evil.
19.7: All the brethren of the poor man hate him and his friends are far removed from him; he that is malicious in his words is not trustworthy.
19.8: He that gets wisdom loves his soul; he that keeps the faith finds good.
19.9: A false witness will not be spared, and he that speaks lies will be destroyed.
19.10: A delicacy is not fitting for a fool, neither for a Servant to rule among princes.
19.11: The intelligence of a man is his patience and his glory is the passing from evil.
19.12: The wrath of the King roars like a lion, and his pleasure is like the dew upon the grass.
19.13: A foolish son is a disgrace to a father, and the anxiety of a wife is like a continual dripping.
19.14: A house and property is the inheritance of a father and a woman is betrothed to a man from LORD JEHOVAH.
19.15: Slumber gives sleep and the proud soul will suffer hunger.
19.16: He that keeps the commandment takes care of his soul and he that despises his ways will be killed.
19.17: He that follows LORD JEHOVAH shows mercy upon the poor and he is rewarded according to his works.
19.18: Chasten your son because there is hope and do not cast out your soul to his shame.
19.19: An angry man receives injury and as much as he brings in by his care, it increases.
19.20: Hear counsel and receive instruction so that you may be wise in your ways.
19.21: Many are the thoughts in the heart of a man, and the counsel of LORD JEHOVAH will be confirmed.
19.22: The desire of a man is his favor, and the poor man is better than a rich man who lies.
19.23: The awe of LORD JEHOVAH is unto life, and he that is filled with it will live and will not visit evil.
19.24: A lazy man that hides his hands in his chest will not even bring them to his mouth.
19.25: Whenever a fool is whipped, the wise pays attention, and if you rebuke the wise, he perceives knowledge.
19.26: He that plunders his father and grieves his mother is a disgraceful and shameful son.
19.27: Stop, my son, and hear instruction, and do not forget the speech of knowledge.
19.28: An evil witness is loud in judgment, and the mouth of the evil swallows him up.
19.29: Pains are prepared for those that are loud in judgment, and affliction for foolish people.

20.1: Wine is strong and drunkenness is disgraceful, and no one that is excessive in it is wise.
20.2: The wrath of the King roars like a lion and he that angers him sins against himself.
20.3: It is the glory of a man whenever he separates a dispute, and every fool mocks it.
20.4: A lazy man is reproached and does not keep silent; he begs in harvest and there is no water.
20.5: The word in the heart of the King is deep, and a wise man will draw it out.
20.6: Many of the sons of men are called compassionate men, but who finds a trustworthy man?
20.7: Blessed after him are the children of the righteous one who walks in integrity.
20.8: The King sits upon the throne of judgment and all the wicked are scattered before him.
20.9: Who will say, "You are innocent, my heart, and I am purified from sin"?
20.10: Discrepant weights and discrepant measures, both are despised before LORD JEHOVAH.
20.11: Even by his work a boy is known, if he is pure, and if his works are right.
20.12: The ear that hears and the eye that sees; LORD JEHOVAH has made them.
20.13: Do not love sleep, lest you become poor, but open your eyes and be satisfied with bread.
20.14: A friend may say to his companion, "I have acquired wealth”, and then he will be praised.
20.15: But let him say, "Here is gold and a multitude of precious stones and valuable vessels, and the lips of knowledge.”
20.16: Take the cloak of one that pledges security for a foreigner, and for the sake of foreigners take his pledge.
20.17: He pledges security to a man with deceitful bread, and after that, his mouth will be filled with pebbles.
20.18: Counsel is acquired in the mind and in provocation war is made.
20.19: He that reveals a secret is a slanderer, and he that is faithful in his spirit hides a matter; and do not associate with one whose lips are hasty.
20.20: He that curses his father and his mother will extinguish his lamp as dark pupils.
20.21: An inheritance that is hasty in its beginning will not be blessed in its end.
20.22: Do not say, "I shall pay evil instead of good”, but wait for LORD JEHOVAH that he may save you.
20.23: Discrepant weights are defiled before LORD JEHOVAH and deceitful scales are not good.
20.24: The steps of a man are before LORD JEHOVAH, and who is the man that will order his way?
20.25: It is a snare unto a man who vows holiness and whose soul is sorry for it after he vows.
20.26: The wise King scatters evil ones and turns a wheel over them.
20.27: The soul of the children of men is the lamp of LORD JEHOVAH, and it searches all the inner rooms of the belly.
20.28: Grace and truth keep the King and his throne is established by grace.
20.29: The glory of young men is their strength and the excellence of elders is old age.
20.30: Misery and torment fall upon the evil, and affliction into the inner rooms of the belly.

21.1: The heart of the King is like a stream of waters in the hands of God, and he turns it wherever he chooses.
21.2: All the ways of a man are right in his own eyes, and LORD JEHOVAH sets the heart in order.
21.3: Whoever does righteousness and judgment is chosen to LORD JEHOVAH by sacrifice.
21.4: The pride of the eyes and the spirit of the heart and the lamp of the evil is sin.
21.5: The thoughts of the elect one are trusting, and those of the evil one cause want.
21.6: The effect of a treasure by a lying tongue is even unto destruction; those that seek death will be overthrown.
21.7: The shipwreck of the evil will come upon them, because they did not choose to do justice.
21.8: He that perverts his way is a strange man, and the works of the pure one are right.
21.9: It is better to sit on the corner of the roof than to dwell with a contentious woman.
21.10: The soul of the evil is not apparent to the eyes of his neighbors.
21.11: By the injury of the evil the child is made wise, and by his reflection the wise receives knowledge.
21.12: The Righteous One understands the heart of the evil, and he drives out the evil ones unto affliction.
21.13: He that shuts his ears that he will not hear the poor, he also will call out to God, and he will not answer him.
21.14: A gift in secret removes wrath and he that spares giving arouses anger.
21.15: The joy of the righteous works justice, and ruin to evildoers.
21.16: A son of man that strays from the way of understanding will descend with the children of the Earth.
21.17: A man that is wanting and loves joy and wine and pleasure, will not be rich.
21.18: The evil is an exchange for the righteous and a liar for the upright.
21.19: Better to stay in the land of the wilderness than to dwell with a contentious and angry woman.
21.20: There is coveted treasure and oil in the dwelling of a wise man and the intelligent of the children of men will consume it.
21.21: He that seeks righteousness and grace finds life, righteousness and glory.
21.22: A wise man went up to the city of a mighty man and conquered its trusted fortress.
21.23: He that keeps his mouth and his tongue keeps his soul from trouble.
21.24: An insolent one whose name is mighty does evil in his wrath.
21.25: The lust of the lazy one kills him, because he does not choose to work with his hands.
21.26: He desires lust all day long; the righteous gives and does not spare.
21.27: The sacrifice of the evil ones is detested before LORD JEHOVAH because they bring it in evil.
21.28: A false witness will be destroyed and an attentive man speaks justly.
21.29: The face of an evil man is shameless, and he who is upright establishes the ways of his soul.
21.30: There is no wisdom, neither intelligence, nor mind, like that of LORD JEHOVAH.
21.31: The horse is prepared for the day of war, but salvation is of LORD JEHOVAH.

22.1: The Name is better than great wealth, and compassion is better than gold or silver.
22.2: The rich and the poor meet each other and LORD JEHOVAH made both of them.
22.3: A prudent one sees an evil man being scourged and is powerfully instructed, but fools pass by him and suffer damage.
22.4: The pursuers of humility: the awe of LORD JEHOVAH, wealth, honor and Life.
22.5: Nets and snares are in the perverse way, and he that keeps his soul will be far from them.
22.6: He that reproves a boy concerning the beginning of his way, even if he becomes old, he will not turn away from it.
22.7: A rich man will be authorized by the poor and the Servant will lend to him who was his lender.
22.8: He that sows evil will reap fraud, and the scepter of his anger will be destroyed.
22.9: Whose ever eye is good will be blessed, because he gives of his bread to the poor.
22.10: Destroy the evil one and cast out the contentious, abolish judgment and shame, lest he sit in the assembly and shame all of you.
22.11: God loves him that is pure in heart and he shows love upon the lips of the king's friends.
22.12: The eyes of LORD JEHOVAH preserve knowledge and they destroy the words of liars.
22.13: When the lazy man was sent, he said, "There is a lion in the road, and look, a killer is in the streets!"
22.14: The mouth of an estranged woman is a deep ditch, and he with whom LORD JEHOVAH is angry falls there.
22.15: Senselessness disturbs the heart of a boy and a rod of discipline puts it far from him.
22.16: He that harms the poor increases his own affliction and he that gives to the rich suffers loss to his soul.
22.17: Bend your ear and hear the words of the wise and apply your heart to knowledge.
22.18: Because they are sweet, keep them in your gut, and they will form together upon your lips.
22.19: I have informed you today that your trust may be upon LORD JEHOVAH.
22.20: And look, I have written these things to you three times
22.21: That I may make known to you counsel, knowledge, stillness and the words of truth, that you may return the word of truth to him that sends you.
22.22: Do not oppress the poor because he is poor and do not humiliate the afflicted one in the gate.
22.23: Because LORD JEHOVAH decides their judgment and the humiliation of their souls is repaid.
22.24: You shall not be a friend to an angry man and do not enter with a man that is filled with passion,
22.25: Lest you learn his laws and you find a scandal to your soul.
22.26: Do not give yourself as security because you are shamed before people.
22.27: If you cannot pay, they will take the bed from under you.
22.28: Do not remove the border which your fathers have made from old times.
22.29: If you have seen a man skilled in his work, he will stand before kings and he will not stand in front of obscure men.

23.1: If you sit to dine with a Ruler, perceive what is set before you
23.2: Lest you put a dagger to your mouth, if you are a man that breathes.
23.3: And do not lust for his food because his bread is the bread of lies.
23.4: Do not come near a rich man, but in wisdom, separate from him;
23.5: For if you fix your eye on him, he disappears before you, because he makes wings for himself like an eagle and flies into the sky.
23.6: Do not dine with a gluttonous man and do not lust for his food.
23.7: For just as a man swallows a bristle hair, so you eat and drink with him and his mind is not with you.
23.8: And the bread that you eat, you vomit, and you spoil your sweet words.
23.9: Do not speak in front of a fool because he despises your words.
23.10: Do not remove the boundary which is of old, and do not enter into the field of orphans
23.11: Because their Savior is strong and he shall judge their case with you.
23.12: Bring your heart to instruction and your ear to the speech of knowledge.
23.13: Do not withhold discipline from a boy, because if you strike him, he does not die.
23.14: For you strike him with a rod and you deliver his soul from Sheol.
23.15: My son, if your heart is wise, my heart will also rejoice.
23.16: And my kidneys will be glad whenever your lips speak uprightness.
23.17: Let not your heart envy sinners but be zealous for the awe of LORD JEHOVAH all day long.
23.18: For there will be a posterity for you, and your hope will not end.
23.19: Hear, my son, and be wise, and set up my doctrine in your heart.
23.20: Do not be drunk with wine and do not be a glutton of meat.
23.21: Because he that is drunk with wine and a glutton of meat will be destroyed, and he that sleeps will wear horse cloth.
23.22: Hear your father who begot you and do not despise the old age of your mother.
23.23: Buy truth and do not sell wisdom, instruction or understanding.
23.24: The father of the righteous will exult and leap for joy, and he that begets a wise son, and his father will rejoice in him.
23.25: And your father and your mother will rejoice in you and she that bore you will rejoice.
23.26: Give me your heart, my son, and your eyes will keep my ways.
23.27: For a whore is a deep ditch and an estranged woman is a disturbed well.
23.28: She destroys suddenly and many evil men multiply.
23.29: Who has woe? Who has trouble? Who has judgment and who has afflictions? Who has wounds without cause and who has red eyes?
23.30: Is it not those that linger at the wine and track down where there is a tavern? You shall not be drunk with wine, but speak with righteous men and walk and talk with them.
23.31: Do not fix your eyes on the wine that is red, its color in the cup, but meditate on righteousness.
23.32: Because the end of the wine is like a wound from a serpent and like a flying cockatrice.
23.33: When your eyes gaze at an estranged woman, then your heart speaks perverse things.
23.34: And you yourself are like he that lies down in the heart of the sea, and like a sailor in a great storm.
23.35: And you will say, "They hit me and I did not suffer; they were abusive to me and I did not know it. When I wake up and I go out, I will seek it.”

24.1: Do not envy evil men and do not lust to be with them.
24.2: Because they devise evil in their heart, and their lips speak evil.
24.3: A house is built by wisdom and by understanding it is entirely put in good order.
24.4: And the inner chambers are filled by knowledge with every possession, honor and delight.
24.5: The wise is better than the strong, and a man of knowledge than a mighty man of power.
24.6: War is made in provocation, and salvation in the abundance of counsel.
24.7: Wisdom casts out the fool and he does not open his mouth in the gate.
24.8: He that devises to do evil is called an evil man.
24.9: The counsel of the fool is sin; the defilement of a son of man is evil.
24.10: Affliction will drive the evil in the day of distress.
24.11: Deliver those that are being led to death and do not spare to save those that are being held to kill.
24.12: And if you say, "I did not know it”, know that God examines the thought of the heart, and he who keeps your soul knows, and rewards a son of man according to his works.
24.13: Eat honey, my son, because it is good, and honeycomb is sweet to your palate.
24.14: So also you will find wisdom to your soul, and it will be the end to you, and your hope will not be cut off.
24.15: Do not plot evil in the dwelling of the righteous and do not plunder the place of his dwelling.
24.16: Because the righteous falls seven times, and he rises, and the wicked is overthrown by evil.
24.17: When your enemy has fallen, do not rejoice, and when he is overthrown, let not your heart leap for joy.
24.18: Lest LORD JEHOVAH see, and it is evil in his eyes, and he turn his wrath from him.
24.19: Do not emulate evil ones and do not envy the evil.
24.20: Because there is no posterity to the evil ones and the lamp of the evil will be extinguished.
24.21: Stand in awe, my son, of LORD JEHOVAH, and give counsel, and do not mingle with fools.
24.22: Their shipwreck comes suddenly, and who knows the end of their years?
24.23: I say these things to the wise; to accept persons in judgment is not good
24.24: Neither to say to the evil, "You are righteous"; the nations will curse such, and the peoples will revile him.
24.25: And the reprovers will be delighted, and a good blessing will come upon them.
24.26: And the lips will kiss those that reprove.
24.27: Prepare your works in the outdoors and make ready in your field, and after that build your house.
24.28: Do not be a false witness against your neighbor and do not tear him to pieces with your lips.
24.29: And do not say, "I shall do to him as he does to me and I shall pay him according to his works.”
24.30: I passed by the field of a lazy man and by the vineyard of a son of man lacking heart.
24.31: And all of that climbed up, filled with a tangle of thorns, and they covered the face of the gates.
24.32: And I saw and I set my heart, and I received instruction.
24.33: And if you slumber a little and sleep a little, and for a little you put your hand upon your chest
24.34: Poverty will walk before you, and want will overtake you like a man who is a runner.

25.1: These also are the deep proverbs of Solomon which the friends of Hezekiah king of Judah wrote:
25.2: It is the glory of God that hides the word, and the glory of the King that seeks for the word.
25.3: The Heavens are high and the Earth deep, and the heart of the King is unsearchable.
25.4: Select dross from silver so that a pure item may come forth.
25.5: Evil ones will be scattered from before the King and he will establish his throne in righteousness.
25.6: Do not boast before the king, and do not stand in the place of great men.
25.7: For it is better for one to say to you, "Come up”, than that you be humbled in front of a Ruler. Whatever your eyes see, that tell.
25.8: Do not go out for anything hastily, lest when you bring a lawsuit, your friend will revile you at the end.
25.9: Judge your judgment with your neighbor and do not reveal the secret of another
25.10: Lest he that hears will revile you, and many complain against you.
25.11: He that speaks the word is an apple of gold in a vessel of beaten silver.
25.12: An earring of gold and precious Sardius, so is the reproof of the wise to the ear of the hearer.
25.13: As whenever falling snow in a day of harvest cools the heat, so is a faithful messenger to his senders, and the soul of his Masters returns.
25.14: As when there are clouds and wind without rain, so is a man that boasts of false gifts.
25.15: In patience soothe a Ruler, and with a soft tongue break bones.
25.16: Whenever you have found honey, eat enough for you, lest when you are full, you vomit it.
25.17: Let not your foot frequent the house of your friend, lest he be weary of you and hate you.
25.18: Like a club, a sword and a sharp arrow, so is a man that testifies false testimony against his neighbor.
25.19: Like a bad tooth and a foot out of joint, so is trust in a liar in the day of trouble.
25.20: He that takes the cloak from his neighbor in the day of cold as like he that casts dirt upon wealth, and to chastise a grieved heart is like a moth to a garment and like a boring worm to a tree; so grief wounds the heart of a man.
25.21: If your enemy hungers, feed him, and if he thirsts, give him drink.
25.22: And when you do these things to him, you welcome coals of fire upon his head, and LORD JEHOVAH rewards you.
25.23: As the North wind gives birth to rain, so also is an evil face to a lurking tongue.
25.24: It is better to dwell on the corner of a roof than to dwell with a contentious woman in a house of divisions.
25.25: Like cold waters to a thirsty soul, so is good news that comes from a distant land.
25.26: Like a stopped up fountain and a stream of sewage, so is a righteous one that falls down before an evil one.
25.27: To eat much honey is not good, neither to seek words of praise.
25.28: Like a city breach without a wall, so is an impatient man.

26.1: Like snow in summer and like rain in harvest, so is honor unbecoming for a fool.
26.2: Like a bird and a flying creature in the air, so an empty curse wanders.
26.3: A whip for a horse and a goad for a donkey, so also is a rod for the fool.
26.4: Do not give answer to a fool according to his foolishness, lest you also be like him.
26.5: But speak with a fool according to your wisdom lest he think in his soul that he is wise.
26.6: He that sends a message by the hand of a fool is asking for trouble.
26.7: If you make a cripple walk, you may take the word of a fool.
26.8: Like a stone in a sling, so is he that praises a fool.
26.9: Thorns are sprung up in the hand of a drunkard and madness in the mouth of the fool.
26.10: The flesh of the fool suffers much and a drunkard crosses the sea.
26.11: Like a dog that returns to its vomit, so is a fool that is insane in his foolishness.
26.12: If you have seen a man that is wise in his own eyes, a fool is more useful than he.
26.13: Whenever a lazy man is sent, he says, "A lion is in the road"; "A lion is in the midst of the streets.”
26.14: Like a door that swings around on its hinges, so the lazy man turns on his bed.
26.15: The lazy one that hides his hands in his chest will labor to bring them to his mouth.
26.16: The lazy one is wiser in his own eyes than seven men who make sense.
26.17: He that meddles in a case that is not his is like he that takes a dog by its ears.
26.18: Just as a braggart casts words like sharp arrows of death
26.19: So is the man that deceives his neighbor, and when it is known, says, "I was just joking.”
26.20: When there is no wood, the fire is extinguished, and where there is no troublemaker, strife ceases.
26.21: A gridiron for coals and wood for fire, and a malicious man provokes dispute.
26.22: The words of the malicious stir up strife and they go down to the inner chambers of the heart.
26.23: Like tried silver plated over a clay vessel, so are fiery lips and an evil heart.
26.24: By his lips a hateful man is known, and he plots an ambush within him.
26.25: And if he makes his voice gentle, do not believe him, because seven evils are within him.
26.26: The evil of him that hides hatred in his heart will be revealed in the assembly.
26.27: He that digs a ditch will fall into it, and he that rolls a stone, it will return upon him.
26.28: A lying tongue hates the truth and the malicious mouth works trouble.

27.1: Do not boast about tomorrow, for you do not know what it brings forth.
27.2: Let a stranger praise you, and not your own mouth, and another, and not your own lips.
27.3: Stone is heavy and sand is weighty, and the wrath of the fool is heavier than both.
27.4: Presumption is rage and impetuosity is passion, but who shall stand before envy?
27.5: Public rebuke is better than secret love.
27.6: The wounds of a friend are better than the kisses of an enemy.
27.7: The soul that is full treads on honeycombs, and even bitter things are sweet to the hungry soul.
27.8: Like a bird that leaves its nest, so is a man that is removed from his place.
27.9: As oil and fragrances give joy to the heart, so is he that is sweet to his neighbor in the counsel of his soul.
27.10: Do not forsake your friend or the friend of your father, and do not enter the house of your brother in the day of your brokenness; better is a neighbor that is near than the brother that is distant.
27.11: Be wise my son, and gladden my heart, and remove from me the reproach of those who reproach me.
27.12: A cunning man sees evil and is hidden, but fools have passed by it and have suffered loss.
27.13: The cloak of him who is security for a stranger is taken, and for the person of a stranger he pledges it.
27.14: He that blesses his neighbor with flattery in a loud voice is not different from him that pronounces a curse.
27.15: Like a dripping that drips abundantly on a day of heavy rain, so is a contentious woman.
27.16: The North wind is severe, and it is called by name "Propitious.”
27.17: Iron sharpens iron, and a man sharpens the person of his neighbor.
27.18: He that keeps a fig tree will eat of its fruit and he that is watchful of his Master will be praised.
27.19: As faces are not alike, so hearts are not alike.
27.20: Sheol and destruction are not full, neither are the eyes of men satisfied.
27.21: A refining pot proves silver, and a furnace: gold, so is a man proven by the mouth of his praisers. The heart of the evil seeks evil, and the heart of the pure seeks knowledge.
27.22: If you strike a fool in the assembly, you do not help him, neither do you remove his foolishness.
27.23: Whenever you shepherd, know the faces of your sheep, and set your heart upon your flock.
27.24: Because dominion is not forever, neither is it delivered generation to generation.
27.25: Grass sprouts and new grass appears, and the green herb is gathered from the mountains.
27.26: Lambs for your clothes and kids for your food
27.27: And the milk of goats for your food and for the food of your house.

28.1: The evil will flee when there is no one chasing them, and the righteous are as confident as a lion.
28.2: Among the evil of the Earth are its many Rulers, and the righteous children of men who know righteousness endure.
28.3: A poor man that harms the poor is like a beating rain with no benefit in it.
28.4: Those that forsake the Law praise evil and those that keep the Law strengthen their souls.
28.5: Evil men do not understand judgment, and they that seek LORD JEHOVAH know all good things.
28.6: The poor walks in integrity and the rich perverts his way.
28.7: He that keeps the law is an intelligent son and he that serves worthlessness shames his father.
28.8: He that increases his property by contract and from usury will leave it to him that shows mercy upon the poor.
28.9: He that shuts his ears that he will not hear the Law, even his prayer is defiled.
28.10: He that leads an upright one astray into the way of evil will fall into a ditch and the innocent will inherit good things.
28.11: The rich man is wise in his own eyes and a poor intelligent one condemns him.
28.12: By the strength of the righteous ones, glory increases, and in the rise of the evil ones it is diminished.
28.13: A son of man that will hide his evil will not succeed, and God will be moved with compassion upon him that confesses to his sins and passes from them.
28.14: Blessed is the man that senses awe always, and he that hardens his heart will fall into evil.
28.15: The lion roars and the bear has given a sound because an evil one rules over poor people.
28.16: Many are the griefs of the Ruler lacking a mind and whoever hates deceit prolongs his days.
28.17: A son of man that is guilty of the blood of a soul will flee unto the elected ones and they will not help him.
28.18: He that walks without blemish will be redeemed and he that perverts his ways will fall into a pit.
28.19: He that cultivates the soil will be satisfied with bread and he that runs after worthlessness will be filled with poverty.
28.20: Many are the blessings of a faithful man and an evil one will not be spared in his evil.
28.21: A man that favors persons is not good, for by a piece of bread he betrays a man.
28.22: A man with an evil eye hastens to be rich and he does not know that loss has come upon him.
28.23: He that rebukes a son of man finds more favor than he that is duplicitous with his tongue.
28.24: He that plunders his father and his mother and says, "I have no sin”, is a partner with an evil man.
28.25: The covetous man stirs up contention and he that is assured by LORD JEHOVAH will grow fat.
28.26: He that trusts upon his own heart is a fool and he that walks in integrity will be delivered.
28.27: He that gives to the poor will not lack it, and he that turns his eyes from the poor will increase his curse.
28.28: In the rise of the evil a son of man is hidden, and in their destruction the righteous will be multiplied.

29.1: The man that does not receive rebuke and hardens his neck will quickly be ruined and there will be no healing for him.
29.2: By the multitude of the righteous, the people increase, and by the authority of the evil, the people will groan.
29.3: A man that loves wisdom gladdens his father and he that traffics with prostitution will destroy his possessions.
29.4: The king by judgment establishes the land, and the evil one deprives it.
29.5: A man that is duplicitous with his neighbor spreads a net for his steps.
29.6: An evil man is caught in his evil and the righteous will sing and rejoice.
29.7: For the righteous knows the justice of the poor and the evil does not recognize knowledge.
29.8: Men that are mockers burn up fortress cities and the wise turn back wrath.
29.9: A wise man debates with a fool, and he is passionate and laughs and is not enraged.
29.10: Men that are shedders of blood hate The Pure One and the righteous love him.
29.11: The fool expresses all his anger and the wise thinks with his mind.
29.12: All the Servants of a Prince that hears a lying word are evil.
29.13: The poor and the oppressor have met each other, and LORD JEHOVAH enlightens the eyes of both.
29.14: The throne of the King that judges in truth will be established in justice.
29.15: A rod and reproof give wisdom and a boy without discipline shames his mother.
29.16: In the abundance of the evil ones, evil increases, and the righteous will rejoice in their fall.
29.17: Discipline your son, and he will comfort you, and delight your soul.
29.18: In the multitude of the evil ones a people is breached, and he that keeps the law is blessed.
