29.19: A Servant is not disciplined by words, for he knows that he will not be beaten.
29.20: If you have seen a man hasty in his words, know that a fool is better than he.
29.21: He that is pampered from his childhood will be a Servant, and at his end he will groan.
29.22: An angry man stirs up judgment and a furious man increases evil.
29.23: The pride of a son of man will humiliate him and his humility will increase honor to him.
29.24: He that partakes with the thief hates his soul and they put him under oath and he does not confess.
29.25: The evil of a man makes him a scandal and he that trusts upon LORD JEHOVAH will prevail.
29.26: Many seek the face of a Ruler and the judgment of a man proceeds from LORD JEHOVAH.
29.27: An evil man is defiled to the righteous, and the right way is defiled to the evil ones.

30.1: The words of Agur, son of Yaqay, who received the prophecy and was visited by power and he said to Ithliel:
30.2: Because my thoughts are deficient and I do not have the understanding of the children of men,
30.3: And I have not known wisdom neither have I learned the knowledge of The Holy Ones,
30.4: He said to me: "Who went up to Heaven and came down, and who is holding the wind in his palms? Who has bound the waters in a napkin and who has established all the ends of the Earth? What is his Name, and what is his Son's Name, if you know?"
30.5: All the words of God are chosen and he helps those that trust upon him.
30.6: Do not add to his words, lest he rebuke you, and you be proven a liar.
30.7: I have requested two things of you; do not deny me them before I die.
30.8: Take emptiness and lying speech far from me, and do not give me poverty or wealth. Give me a living sufficient for me.
30.9: Lest I be full and I lie and say, "Who is LORD JEHOVAH?”, or lest I become poor and I steal and swear in the name of my God.
30.10: Do not hand over a Servant to his Master, lest he curse you and you be condemned
30.11: To a generation that its fathers will revile and its mother will not bless;
30.12: A generation that is pure in its own eyes and is not washed from its own filthiness;
30.13: A generation: how haughty are its eyes and its eyelids raised!
30.14: A generation whose swords are its teeth, and daggers its molars, to consume the poor of earth and the afflicted of men.
30.15: Three leeches have three beloved daughters that are not full, and a fourth does not say, "It is enough":
30.16: Sheol and her barren womb, the ground that is not filled with water, and fire does not say, "It is enough.”
30.17: Ravens of the valley will tear out the eye that laughs at its father and despises the old age of its mother, and the young Eagles will eat it.
30.18: Three things are hidden from me, and a fourth I have not known exists:
30.19: The way of an eagle in the sky, the way of the snake upon a rock, the way of the ship in the heart of the sea and the way of a man in his youth.
30.20: So is the way of an adulterous woman; she eats and she wipes her mouth and she says, "I have done nothing.”
30.21: The Earth quakes under three things, and under four, it cannot endure:
30.22: Under a Servant when he will reign, and under a fool when he is filled with bread,
30.23: And under a hateful woman that is married to a man, and under a Maidservant that dismisses her mistress.
30.24: Four things are small in the Earth, and they are wiser than the wise:
30.25: Ants that have no power in them and from the harvest they prepare their bread,
30.26: And the rabbits that are lacking in power and make themselves houses in the rocks,
30.27: And locusts that have no King over them and they all assemble together,
30.28: And the lizard that clings with its hands and dwells in Kings' houses.
30.29: There are three that are right in their walking and the fourth is beautiful walking:
30.30: The whelp of a lion is champion of all beasts, neither is he afraid nor does he turn from any beast,
30.31: And a rooster that struts among hens, and the hegoat that goes at the head of the flock, and the King that speaks among the peoples.
30.32: Do not covet, lest you be despised, and do not stretch your hand to your mouth in depravity.
30.33: From the fat of the milk butter comes forth, but if you squeeze your hand upon wheat, blood will come forth, so from contention among people judgment goes forth.

31.1: The words of Moayl, The King, the Prophet, which his mother taught him and she said to him:
31.2: Oh, my son, oh, son of my body, and oh, son of my vows!
31.3: Do not give your strength to women and your ways for the provisions of Kings
31.4: Beware of Kings, Moayl, of Kings that drink wine, and of Rulers that drink strong drink
31.5: Lest you drink and you forget the Prophet, and you forget the judgment of all the poor children.
31.6: Strong drink is given to mourners and wine to bitter souls,
31.7: That they may drink and they may forget their sorrows, and their miseries would not be remembered anymore.
31.8: Open your mouth with the word of truth and judge all the sons of evil.
31.9: Open your mouth in righteous judgment and judge the poor and the afflicted.
31.10: Who finds a diligent woman? For she is precious beyond incomparable precious stones.
31.11: The heart of her husband trusts in her and she lacks no provision.
31.12: She has done good things for him and not bad things all the days of her life.
31.13: She has sought wool and fine linen and her hands have worked according to his pleasure.
31.14: She has been like a merchant's ship that brings its merchandise from far away.
31.15: She is risen in the night and has given food to the children in her house, and work to her girls.
31.16: She has looked at a field and bought it, and from the fruit of her hands she planted a vineyard.
31.17: She has girt her waist with strength and has strengthened her arms.
31.18: She has tasted that her merchandise is good and her lamp does not go out all night.
31.19: She stretched out her arms with diligence and her hands to the spindle.
31.20: She has stretched her hands out to the poor and she stretched out her arms to the afflicted.
31.21: The children in her house have not been afraid of the snow, because all of them wear scarlet.
31.22: She has made a carpet for herself and a robe of fine white linen and of purple.
31.23: Her husband is known among the cities as he sits among the Elders of the land.
31.24: She has made and sold fine linen and has given girdles to the Canaanites.
31.25: Strength and excellence are her clothing and she will rejoice in the last day.
31.26: She has opened her mouth in wisdom and the law of compassion is upon her tongue.
31.27: The ways of her house are open and she has not eaten the bread of laziness.
31.28: Her children stand up and give her blessings and her husband praises her.
31.29: And many of her daughters have retained wealth, and you have far surpassed all of these.
31.30: Beauty is false and favor is empty, but a woman that is in the awe of LORD JEHOVAH, she will be praised.
31.31: Give her of the fruit of her hands, and her deeds will praise her in the gate.
